title: Koanophyllon hammatocladum
taxonomy: Koanophyllon hammatocladum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions. As part of the Asteraceae family, Koanophyllon hammatocladum belongs to the broader class of dicotyledonous plants, which have two cotyledons or seed leaves. Its classification also includes the division of flowering plants, known as angiosperms, and the kingdom of plants, which includes all types of plants. intro: Koanophyllon hammatocladum, also known as 'Hamatocladum', is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon hammatocladum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
